content: Monosodium glutamate (MSG) is formed when sodium is added to naturally occurring glutamate found in foods like parmesan, tomatoes, peas, and mushrooms.
MSG increases the umami taste sensation by activating different taste buds and triggering neural responses in the brain.
MSG consumption can mask poor quality or spoiled food, potentially leading to health issues if ingested in large quantities.
A study on mice found that high doses of MSG led to brain cell swelling and death, suggesting potential negative effects on the brain.
Vitamin C, particularly in high doses, has been suggested to help counteract the adverse effects of MSG, such as numbness and dizziness.
Drinking plenty of water is recommended to prevent MSG-induced water retention, though sodium in general is typically responsible for water retention.
